cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Leslie could have gone to the beach. First, let's list out all the times Leslie was seen doing different activities. Leslie woke up at 5am. Then, Emily saw Leslie in class from 5am to 9am. So during that time, Leslie was at school. Then Anthony saw Leslie watching a movie from 9am to 11am. After that, Thomas saw Leslie at the bakery from 11am to 4pm. Then Ashley saw Leslie at the electronics store fixing their computer from 4pm to 5pm. Finally, Susan saw Leslie walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. Now, the beach was closed after 10pm, so Leslie couldn't have gone after that. So the latest possible time would be before 10pm. We need to check the times when Leslie was not accounted for. Let's go step by step: 5am - 9am: At school. So not available. 9am - 11am: At the movie theater. Not available. 11am - 4pm: At the bakery. Still not available. 4pm - 5pm: At the electronics store. Still not available. So from 5pm until when? After 5pm, Leslie is not mentioned. The next activity is at 9pm to 10pm walking towards the statue. So between 5pm and 9pm, Leslie wasn't seen doing anything. But the beach closes at 10pm. So Leslie could have gone to the beach during the time they were unaccounted for, which is 5pm to 9pm. Let me check if that's one of the options. Looking at the options: (A) 5pm to 9pm (B) 9am to 11am (C) 9pm to 10pm (D) 4pm to 5pm Yes, option A is 5pm to 9pm. That's when Leslie wasn't seen doing anything else. Wait, but what about 9pm to 10pm (option C)? Leslie was walking towards the Statue of Liberty then. The beach is closed after 10pm, but maybe Leslie got to the beach before 10pm? But they were seen at 9pm-10pm walking towards the statue. If the beach is closed after 10pm, but Leslie was at the statue from 9-10pm, then maybe they couldn't have gone to the beach during that time. Also, option C is when they were walking to the statue, so they can't be at the beach then. So the only time Leslie was free was from 5pm to 9pm. So the answer should be A. Let me double-check. Yes, from
